evidence.scientific_clarity: Table clearly presents 4 methods x 5 ML benchmarks with group labels (Baselines/Ours); OursModel leads all columns; bold logic and subscript ±std format are readable and correct.
evidence.typography: Main text uses embedded CM Type 1 fonts (CMR10, CMMI10) — selectable and scalable — but three Type 3 (bitmap) font objects (F44, F41, F45) are also present, likely for math/symbol glyphs; pdf.fonttype=42 is matplotlib-specific and N/A for pdfLaTeX.
evidence.palette: Table uses no color (appropriate for a comparison table); bold is the sole directional signal for best-per-column, which is unambiguous and colorblind-safe.
evidence.layout_density: Page trimmed to 7x3in via geometry package; group midrules and \quad indentation fill space well; no gaping whitespace or label collisions observed.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption correctly states bold convention and n=5 seeds; 'OursModel achieves highest on every benchmark' matches data; however table.tex (required standalone source) is absent from the working directory.
evidence.vector_fidelity: pdftotext extracts all cell values correctly, confirming selectable text; pdfTeX producer and Type 1 CM fonts confirm real vector output; Type 3 font objects (F44/F41/F45) are a minor flaw.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (60K) and figure.png (78K) and caption.tex and gen_figure.py are all present and non-trivial; table.tex (explicitly required by brief as standalone compilable source) is missing.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption names all five benchmarks by category, states mean±std over n=5 seeds, explains the dual-bold overlap rule, and closes with a concrete directional claim ('highest score on every benchmark').

overall_strengths: Proper booktabs structure (\toprule/\midrule/\bottomrule, no vertical rules), group-spanning rows, and subscript ±std formatting meet camera-ready standards.
Caption is self-contained: bold convention, seed count, and a specific finding are all present, requiring no cross-referencing with the main text.
overall_weaknesses: table.tex — the required standalone LaTeX source that must compile with pdflatex — is absent from the output directory, failing a hard brief requirement.
Three Type 3 (bitmap) font objects appear in the PDF alongside the Type 1 CM fonts; these do not scale cleanly and may trigger journal submission warnings.
